message: bd-8qrt: validate sys_chunk keys and mirror stripes

 {"id":"bd-85ja","title":"Implement btrfs transparent decompression (ZLIB + ZSTD)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-22T18:00:35.647767488Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T18:00:49.704792242Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T18:00:49.704020800Z","close_reason":"Implemented btrfs transparent decompression in the read path:
- Added btrfs_decompress() dispatcher for ZLIB (type 1) and ZSTD (type 3) via flate2 and zstd crates
- LZO (type 2) returns clear UnsupportedFeature error (custom framing format, deferred)
- Updated both inline and regular extent read paths to decompress instead of rejecting
- Added ram_bytes field to BtrfsExtentData enum (Inline and Regular variants) for decompressed size tracking
- Updated parse_extent_data to read ram_bytes from offset 8 of extent item
- Updated to_bytes serialization to write ram_bytes correctly
- Fixed 10 BtrfsExtentData construction sites across ffs-btrfs and ffs-core
- Updated 2 tests from expect-unsupported to expect-corruption (invalid compression data detection)
471 ffs-core tests + 117 ffs-btrfs tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","compression","parity"]}
 {"id":"bd-89x","title":"Docs: Align toolchain/MSRV statements (Rust 2024, nightly toolchain)","description":"Goal: ensure docs do not state an impossible MSRV.

Context:
- Rust edition 2024 requires Rust >= 1.85.
- The repo uses rust-toolchain.toml for nightly.

Deliverables:
- Ensure PLAN/README/COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC consistently state the toolchain policy.
- If we claim an MSRV, it must be technically compatible with edition + dependencies.

Acceptance:
- No doc claims a Rust version that cannot compile the workspace.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:35:11.239312918Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:06:53.885173467Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:06:53.885096573Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-89x","depends_on_id":"bd-2ds","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:35:19.764294386Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-8fpo","title":"ffs-cli evidence streaming parser fails on non-UTF8 torn lines","description":"Fresh-eyes audit found behavior regression: load_evidence_records used BufRead::lines and could return InvalidData on non-UTF8 corrupted lines, unlike prior tolerant parser behavior. Fixed by byte-wise read_until + UTF-8 fallible decode + skip malformed lines; added regression test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-23T01:41:55.499849552Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-23T01:41:55.499849552Z","closed_at":"2026-02-23T01:41:55.499849552Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","reliability"]}
+{"id":"bd-8qrt","title":"Validate btrfs sys_chunk keys and mirror stripe counts","description":"Add validation for sys_chunk_array key type/objectid and enforce mirror-stripe count/device rules in logical mapping","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T12:35:40.426042231Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T12:47:03.822304554Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T12:47:03.822099727Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-8tr","title":"ext4: Implement inode location math helper (inode -> table block + offset)","description":"Goal: given (inode_number, superblock fields, group desc), compute where the inode bytes live on disk.

Deliverables:
- Pure helper: inode_number -> (group, index_in_group).
- Use group desc inode_table pointer and inode_size to compute:
  - table_start_block
  - inode_byte_offset_from_table_start
  - absolute byte offset on device
- Handle ext4 inode numbering rules (inode numbers start at 1; 0 invalid).

Acceptance:
- Unit tests for boundary inodes: 1, inodes_per_group, inodes_per_group+1, last inode.
- Overflow/invalid inputs produce ParseError/FfsError (no panics).
- Helper is used by higher-level inode read code (ffs-inode later).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:17:15.392537885Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:37:56.483745546Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:37:56.483718756Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-8tr","depends_on_id":"bd-2w9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:18:06.687704093Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-907l","title":"Apply worker_threads to FUSE mount queue settings","description":"MountOptions.worker_threads is currently informational only. Map explicit worker_threads values into mount options via max_background and congestion_threshold so it changes kernel-side request queue behavior.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-22T19:24:30.145734334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T19:25:24.881117003Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T19:25:24.820870847Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuse","mount","performance"],"comments":[{"id":291,"issue_id":"bd-907l","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Mapped explicit MountOptions.worker_threads to kernel FUSE queue tuning options in build_mount_options: max_background=<threads> and congestion_threshold=<3/4 threads>. Added tests build_mount_options_includes_queue_tuning_when_worker_threads_explicit and build_mount_options_auto_worker_threads_omits_queue_tuning. Validation: cargo fmt; cargo fmt --check; targeted new tests; cargo check/clippy -p ffs-fuse --all-targets; cargo test -p ffs-fuse (full crate) all pass.","created_at":"2026-02-22T19:25:24Z"}]}
